subject: Biology
title: Mangroves: Trees in the Sea
description: Learn Mangroves: Trees in the Sea in one minute.

hook.text: Mangroves are trees that grow in saltwater—an environment that would kill most other plants.

buildup.text: Their tangled root systems rise above water, anchoring shorelines and sheltering young fish.

discovery.text: Mangroves filter salt through their roots and excrete it through their leaves.

twist.text: Mangrove forests store up to four times more carbon per hectare than tropical rainforests.

climax.text: They protect coastlines from storms and tsunamis—natural barriers worth billions in damage prevention.

punchline.text: Trees that drink saltwater and guard the coast.

quiz.question: How do mangroves handle salt?
options: They filter it through roots and excrete it through leaves
They only grow in low-salt areas
